nativeRange.habitat: Coastal lagoons and sheltered coral reefs at depths of roughly 2-30 m, perching and grazing among rocks and rubble.

diet.notes: Primarily an algae grazer; has been documented picking at film algae and short turf on live rock. Generally advised to offer a varied herbivore diet of spirulina-based flakes/pellets, nori sheets clipped to the rock, and occasional small frozen meaty items (mysis, enriched brine shrimp) for variety.

avoidWith: large or aggressive species that may intimidate it
other Ecsenius species in small tanks

summary: Small Western Pacific reef blenny with a dark caudal-base spot, generally considered a peaceful, personable algae grazer suited to nano and small reef tanks.
careNotes: Recognised by a dark spot at the caudal-fin base and a black-and-yellow band below each eye; reaches roughly 5-6 cm. Generally considered peaceful toward unrelated tankmates but can be territorial toward other blennies, so usually kept singly unless the tank is large with well-separated rockwork. Tends to perch on live rock and graze on film and turf algae; supplementing with herbivore prepared foods and nori is commonly recommended to keep condition up once tank algae thin out. Has been known to jump, so a tightly-fitted lid or covered overflows are advised. Generally regarded as reef-safe; isolated reports of nipping at small polyps exist but are uncommon. Source spread on minimum tank size is wide: LiveAquaria suggests around 10 US gal (~38 L), while hobbyist guides and prose cues (territorial perching, need for ample rockwork) support a larger long-term home; rounded cautious value used here is 75 L (~20 US gal).
breedingNotes: Oviparous with distinct pairing; eggs are demersal and adhesive, attached to substrate via adhesive pads, with planktonic larvae. Captive breeding is uncommonly reported for this species.

primary.name: LiveAquaria
primary.notes: Retailer husbandry page; provided tank size (10 gal), diet, basic temperament, and habitat preferences.

additional.notes: Taxonomy (Blenniidae / Blenniiformes), max length 6.0 cm TL, Western Pacific range (Philippines, Indonesia), 2-30 m depth, oviparous biology.
